title: microlepia substrigosa
intro: Microlepia substrigosa, also known as the creeping fern or the creeping maidenhair fern, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Microlepia substrigosa include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically harvested, dried, and processed into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from skin irritations to respiratory infections.
